courses.title: Development of Nursing

courses.description: The aim of this course is to enable students to develop their understanding of the evolution and development of nursing. In particular, it focuses on the development of the philosophy and knowledge base in nursing. Reference to systematic methods of providing humanistic care within the health care delivery systems will be made.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Discuss the historical development and evolution of nursing.
2. Describe the philosophy of nursing.
3. Discuss the concepts and determinants of health and illness.
4. Describe the health care delivery systems.
5. Discuss the major components of nursing process and its utilization in nursing care.
courses.course_syllabus:
The Nursing Profession:
History of nursing
Definition of nursing
Philosophy of nursing
Four major concepts in nursing
Nursing theories and models
Roles of the nurse
Nursing care plan and nursing diagnosis

Concepts of Health and Health Care Delivery Systems:
Concepts of health and illness
Factors influencing health
Health care delivery systems, local and world-wide
Primary health care

Nursing Process - A Problem-Solving Approach in Nursing:
Assessment: comprehensive health assessment of individuals, family, and community
Planning
Implementation
Evaluation

courses.description: This course aims to equip students with the foundational knowledge and essential nursing skills for promoting the health and wellness of clients. Emphasis will be placed on promoting students’ understanding of the application of basic nursing care principles to safely help clients to meet their basic needs of living. Occupational health and infection control will also be included.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Develop basic communication skills.
2. Apply therapeutic and effective communication skills to care for individuals.
3. Understand principles and knowledge underlying the performance of essential nursing skills.
4. Demonstrate proficiency in nursing skills that meets the client basic living needs.
5. Demonstrate the ability to report and document all nursing actions and care delivered.
courses.course_syllabus:
Communication, interviewing and counseling:
Communication theories and application, effective communication and interpersonal relationship, communication with health care team and consumers, skills of negotiation, lobbying, interviewing and counselling.

Essential nursing techniques:
Ensuring a safe and comfortable environment for care, maintenance and promotion of occupational safety and health, documenting and reporting of care.

Assisting clients to meet basic needs of living:
Meeting safety needs, meeting psychosocial needs, maintaining body temperature, promoting hygiene, promoting rest and sleep, facilitating relief from pain, loss and grief, death and dying.

Wound dressing:
Pathophysiology of wounds and wound healing, wound assessment and classification, impact of acute and chronic wounds, principles of wound management and wound dressing procedures.

Skills:
- Basic communication skills
- Initial interview and health history taking
- General head to toe observations
- Measuring and assessing vital signs
- Bedmaking
- Oral and personal hygiene
- Infection control
- Simple wound dressing (wound swab, dressing materials)
- Complicated wound care (drain, packing irrigation, sutures, clips)
- Pain control (patient control analgesia, hot and cold therapy)
- General procedures (admission, transfer and discharge clients, documenting and reporting)

courses.description: This course aims to further equip students with the foundational knowledge and essential nursing skills for promoting the health and wellness of clients. Emphasis will be placed on promoting students’ understanding of the application of basic nursing care principles to safely help clients to meet their basic needs of living. Intravenous therapy and care will also be included.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Understand basic concepts of nutrition and dietetics.
2. Describe nursing responsibilities in preparing and assisting the client to undertake diagnostic procedures.
3. Discuss principles and knowledge underlying the performance of essential nursing skills.
4. Apply the knowledge and problem solving skills to the care of clients with mobility and nutritional needs.
5. Apply the knowledge and problem solving skills to the promotion of sexual health, spiritual health
6. Demonstrate proficiency in nursing skills to meet the needs of clients receiving peripheral intravenous injection and infusion.
courses.course_syllabus:
Major concepts and topics to be covered:
1. Activity and mobility
- Principles of care for immobility and bed confinement.
- Pressure sore assessment and prevention.
- Care of the ambulant client and use of ambulatory devices.
- Principles and techniques of moving and lifting.
- Principles of physiotherapy and occupational therapy.
2. Nutrition and dietetics
- Basic concepts about food and nutrients.
- A balanced diet for growth and optimal health.
- Introduction of therapeutic diets.
- Industrialization , urbanization and changes in food habits.
- Economics in the choice of food, hospital diet.
3. Fluid and nutrition
- Measures to promote a person’s nutritional status, dietary and fluid intake, intake and output record.
- Assessing nutritional needs associated with each stage of the life cycle.
- Factors that influence fluid and nutritional balance, signs of feeding problems.
- Preparing and serving food, feeding technique.
- Enteral / tube feeding.
4. Diagnostic procedures
- Imaging procedures including various types of x-ray examinations, ultrasonography, magnetic resonance imaging and pulmonary function studies.
- Endoscopic examinations.
- Diagnostic procedures for laboratory studies including tissue biopsies, lumbar puncture, aspirations of cavities, endocrine activity tests and collection of specimens.
- Electrographic diagnostic studies including electrocardiogram, electroencephalogram and electromyogram.
5. Promoting sexual health
- Sexuality and client’s health status
- Principles of performing breast self-examination (BSE) and testicular self-examination (TSE)
6. Promoting spiritual health
- Concepts of spirituality and spiritual care
- Assessing spiritual needs
- Nurses’ role in spiritual care
7. Nursing skills
- Prevention, assessment and treatment of pressure sores.
- Range of motion exercise, use of restraints.
- Assisting a client to walk with/ without ambulatory devices.
- Positioning and transfer.
- Specimen collection including vomit, gastric aspirate, blood glucose and cholesterol.
- Assisting diagnostic procedures including abdominal paracentesis, thoracentesis, bone marrow biopsy, liver biopsy and renal biopsy.
- Feeding dependent clients, screen for swallowing difficulties.
- Intake and output, dietary record.
- Nasogastric tube insertion and withdrawal.
- Administering feeding via nasogastric tube, gastrostomy and jejunostomy tube.
- Introducing intravenous (IV) therapy:
- Initiating peripheral IV infusion
- Regulating IV flow rate
- Using volume control devices/infusion pumps
- Adding medication to IV bolus
- Administrating IV bolus
- Irrigating a heparin lock
- Basic principles of venipuncture and blood taking
- Breast self-examination
- Testicular self-examination

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Fundamentals of Nursing III

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 706

courses.description: This course continuously further equips students with the foundational knowledge and essential nursing skills for promoting the health and wellness of clients. Emphasis will be placed on promoting students’ understanding of the application of basic nursing care principles to safely help clients to meet their basic needs of living. Principles of first aid and drug administration will also be included.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Understand principles and knowledge underlying the performance of essential nursing skills.
2. Demonstrate the ability to perform nursing skills that meets the client’s basic living needs.
3. Understand principles and knowledge in first aid management.
4. Demonstrate proficiency in carrying out basic life support.
5. Demonstrate the ability to administer medications.
courses.course_syllabus:
Meeting bowel elimination needs:
- normal bowel patterns, factors affecting bowel elimination and measures to maintain a regular pattern of bowel elimination; observation of stools and collection of specimens; assisting clients to use devices for bowel elimination (bedpans and commodes/sani-chairs); identifying and caring clients with bowel elimination problems (diarrhoea and bowel incontinence); nursing interventions in managing constipation, impaction and flatulence (inserting suppositories, giving enemas and rectal washouts).

- Related nursing skills: giving and removing bedpan/urinal, assisting clients to use lavatory/commode/sani-chairs, observation and collection of faeces, inserting a rectal suppository and administering an enema, changing napkin on adult clients.

Meeting urinary elimination needs:
- factors affecting urinary elimination; problems with urinary elimination; collection of urine specimens and urinalysis with reference to characteristics of urine; care of clients with urinary incontinence and measures to promote continence; care of clients with altered urinary elimination (retention and incontinence) - catheterization and bladder drainage.

- Related nursing skills: Urine testing (glucose, protein, ketones, SG, pH, occult blood), applying a condom drainage system, inserting and removing indwelling/straight catheters for males and females, collecting mid stream urine and catheterized urine specimen.

First Aid:
- Introduction: aims and management principles, casualty assessment, methods of transferring and carrying injured persons, the use of different types of bandage and splints, improvisation of equipment and utilization of available materials.

- First aid management of various conditions: trauma care (wounds, haemorrhage, and shock); burns and scalds; heat/cold injuries; musculoskeletal injuries (sprains, strains, dislocations, and fractures); loss of consciousness, fits and convulsions; bites and stings; foreign bodies; choking, asphyxia, drowning, and poisoning.

Cardiopulmonary resuscitation (basic life support for adults).
- Related nursing skills: basic bandaging, managing bleeding, shock, burns, heat & cold emergencies, water emergencies, poisoning emergencies, bites & stings, musculoskeletal injuries, removing foreign body causing airway obstruction, adult cardiopulmonary resuscitation.

Administration of medications:
- principles of medication administration; basic written language and mathematical processes in drug therapy; routes, methods and techniques; rules and safety measures; ordering, storage, custody, and recording of controlled drugs according to related drug control ordinances; responsibilities of nurses in relation to medications, the rights and responsibilities of clients in relation to taking medications.

- Related nursing skills: administering oral medications, preparing an injectable medication from an ampoule or vial, administering intradermal, subcutaneous, and intramuscular injections, administering topical applications (skin, eye, nasal, ear), administering ophthalmic instillation, care and custody of drugs: storing, checking, recording, and ordering.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Anatomy
courses.description: The course aims to provide students with basic knowledge of human topographical anatomy with particular emphasis on those aspects of special significance in nursing practice. The course will run concurrently with Physiology (NURS1603) and the syllabus will be integrated so that students will be able to correlate structure and function.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Familiarize with the general organization of the organs in the body and how they organize to form a functional system.
2. Learn the importance of the structural relationship and arrangement in the clinical setting.
3. Describe both the macroscopic and microscopic structures of the organs, and relate them to the functions of the organs.
4. Learn the origin and development of the organs and the associated birth defects.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Cell and Tissues
2. Cardiovascular Systems (heart, artery, vein)
3. Clinical Anatomy (cardiovascular system, skin, respiratory system, musculoskeletal system, digestive system, urinary system, endocrine system, reproductive system, nervous system)
4. Blood and Skin
5. Respiratory, musculoskeletal, digestive, urinary, endocrine and reproductive systems, and nervous system (telencephalon, brainstem and cerebellum, cranial nerves, autonomic, pathway and special senses)

courses.title: Physiology

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg LT
William M W Mong Eng Bldg LT

courses.description: The course aims to enhance students’ knowledge and understanding of how the body adapts physiologically to internal and external environmental changes. It focuses on the physiological principles of normal cells, tissue and system functioning which will form the basis for studying health promotion, pathology and nursing care. The course content will be integrated with that offered in Anatomy (NURS1403).
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Describe the action potential of a neuron and understand the physiological role of nerve impulse conduction.
2. Explain how a skeletal muscle contracts and how muscle fatigue is formed.
3. Describe and explain how homeostasis maintains physiological function.
4. Explain how the cardiovascular system regulates the blood flowing through the tissues.
5. Explain how some of the altered functions in the cardiovascular and pulmonary systems. kidneys, gastrointestinal, endocrine, and central nervous system affect the body.
6. Describe the roles of blood in haemostasis and immune defense.
7. Explain how the pulmonary system and the blood transport oxygen to and carbon dioxide from tissues.
8. Describe the processes by how the kidneys regulate the body fluid volume and its composition.
9. Understand how the gastrointestinal system carries out the digestion and absorption of ingested food.
10. Summarize the roles of various hormones and understand the mechanisms of their secretions.
11. Recall the structure of the nervous system and understand how autonomic nervous system affects body functions.
12. Explain how the special senses particularly the eyes and ears function in human.
13. Summarize the general principles on how the tissues and organs work altogether to maintain homeostasis.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. General Physiology
2. Cardiovascular system
3. Hematology
4. Respiration
5. Body Fluid Compartments
6. Renal Physiology
7. Acid Base Physiology
8. Neurophysiology
9. Gastrointestinal Physiology
10. Endocrinology
11. Reproduction

courses.description: The course aims to help students to acquire basic understanding of microbiology. Emphasis will be placed on micro-organisms that are commonly found on humans and those that are potentially pathogenic. Preventive and control measures in the hospital will also be introduced.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Disaster Nursing
courses.description: This elective course aims to introduce students to the concepts of nursing dealing with disasters, disaster preparedness, nursing activities during and after disaster, and rebuilding the communities after disaster. Emphasis will be on the assessment and care at time of disaster, especially for vulnerable groups such as the elderly, mentally ill, pregnant women and children.
courses.learning_outcomes:
By the end of the course, students will be able to:
1. Apply principles of disaster nursing care to clients during and after disaster.
2. Demonstrate the ability to perform physical, psychological and social care to clients (individuals, families, communities) during disaster.
3. Demonstrate the ability to perform physical, psychological and social care to clients (individuals, families, communities) in post-disaster period.
4. Formulate care to meet the needs of vulnerable populations such as children, disabled and elderly persons after disaster.
5. Integrate legal and ethical concerns in post disaster nursing.
6. Analyse the needs and plan the care for the disaster carers.
courses.course_syllabus:
• The nature of disasters
• Disaster preparation and response system
• Disaster field management
• Chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear disasters
• Psychosocial impact of disasters and their management
• Special populations in disasters
• Public health issues under disaster conditions
• Disaster resilience
• Legal and ethical issues in disaster
• Directions for nursing education, research & development

3. Identify the scope and principles of TCM nursing.
4. Apply TCM nursing principles and skills in health assessment and patient care planning
5. Apply evidence-based knowledge to inform TCM nursing practice.
6. Demonstrate professional nursing roles in coordinating TCM nursing care for clients with the interdisciplinary health care team.
courses.course_syllabus:
(1) Concepts and theories of TCM
 Fundamentals of TCM
 Traditional theory and scientific foundation and evidence of acupuncture
(2) Theoretical principles of TCM nursing and their applications
 Therapeutic principles of TCM nursing
 TCM application in nursing practice
 Exploring TCM practices in nursing research
(3) TCM dialectical nursing
 TCM acupuncture and tuina nursing
 TCM medical and surgical nursing
 TCM nursing care of common diseases

courses.description: The aim of this course is to introduce students to the efficacy, choice, and explanatory mechanisms of complementary and alternative medicine using a range of selected therapies. A regional focus will be on the health concepts and common approaches of Chinese medicine. The roles and responsibilities of nurses within this field are discussed.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Demonstrate an understanding of the underlying concepts of complementary and alternative medicine (CAM).
2. Recognize the integration of Chinese medicine into the health care system of Hong Kong.
3. Determine the roles and responsibilities of nurses in complementary and alternative medicine.
4. Interpret the theories of Chinese Medicine (CM), such as Yin-Yang, the Five Elements, Zang-Fu, Meridians, etc.
5. Describe the special features of diagnosis and treatment in CM.
6. Demonstrate an understanding of dietary therapy from the perspectives of CM.
7. Analyze the major concepts, limitations and special precautions related to a variety of complementary and alternative therapies, including: CM therapies (e.g. acupuncture, moxibustion, tuina, cupping, guasha, bonesetting), homeopathy, aromatherapy, naturopathy, music therapy, therapeutic touch, hypnosis, and chiropractic.
8. Determine the relationship between homuncular reflex theory and its illustration in a number of therapies, including reflexology, auriculotherapy, and iridology.
9. Develop an awareness of certain exercising therapies in Chinese medicine, including: qigong and tai chi.
10. Recognize the role of complementary and alternative medicine as an integrated treatment approach with orthodox Western medicine.
courses.course_syllabus:
- Introduction to complementary & alternative medicine
- The integration of Chinese medicine into the health care system of Hong Kong
- Roles & responsibilities of nurses in CAM
- Introduction to Chinese medicine (Part 1): health concepts of Chinese medicine, five elements theory, internal organs, functions and meridians
- Introduction to Chinese medicine (Part 2): diagnosis and treatment in TCM; principles of dietary therapy
- Acupuncture, moxibustion, tuina, cupping, guasha, bonesetting
- Qigong, tai chi & therapeutic touch; Auricular therapy
- Homeopathy; Reflexology
- Aromatherapy; Naturopathy; Hypnosis; Iridology; Chiropractic; Music therapy
- TCM clinic visit.

courses.description: The aim of this course is to develop students' understanding of the role of the nurse in health promotion by working with individuals, groups, families and communities. Emphasis will be on the application of the principles of health promotion to critique programmes relevant to local current and predicted health needs.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Examine the role of the nurse in undertaking health promotion with individuals, groups, families and communities.
2. Analyse current theories and models of health education and health promotion and their values in the delivery of such programmes.
3. Examine the contribution of health education to health promotion.
4. Analyse the changing pattern of community health and identify priorities and opportunities for health promotion.
5. Assess the learning needs of individuals, families, groups and communities.
6. Apply principles of health promotion to the implementation of programmes.
7. Evaluate the process and outcome of health promotion programmes for both the nurses and clients.

courses.description: This course aims to provide students with the basic understanding of the concepts and theoretical approaches of sociology and psychology relevant to nursing and health care. Emphasis will be on the use of these concepts to understand individual and group behaviour and their health needs.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Examine different approaches to the study of human behavior and their relevance to nursing and/ or health care.
2. Discuss psychosocial needs of individuals at different developmental stages across the lifespan.
3. Discuss the influence of motivation, emotion and individual differences on human behavior.
4. Examine the nature and structure of social groups within the context of Hong Kong such as gender, family, religion, class and work.
5. Discuss the influence of culture, social class, social change, power and politics in relation to the Hong Kong Chinese population.

courses.description: This course will enable students to integrate their knowledge and skills about professional issues into comprehensive understanding of health care. Students will be introduced to health care policy issues and operations of the health care system to further their understanding of health services development. This course introduces students to professionalism, political participation and evidence-based nursing. In addition, the course will prepare students for the use of information technology in the processing of information for nursing practice, research and education.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Examine the factors influencing the future directions of nursing practice.
2. Explain the methods for assuring quality in health care by nurses and the health care team.
3. Compare and contrast different approaches to career ladders in nursing.
4. Examine the challenges in professional development of nursing in Hong Kong.
5. Compare and contrast different health system models.
6. Analyse the challenges of reforming Hong Kong’s health system.
7. Discuss nurses as agents of quality improvement.
8. Analyse the advantages and limitations of using computer applications in the management of
information.
9. Examine the implications of information technology for practice, research and education.
10. Determine and apply the ethical and legal principles in the use of information technology.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Nursing practice and professional issues: Future directions, evidence-based nursing practice, expanded and extended roles of the nurse; clinical career ladders, specialization, advanced nursing practice, nursing audits, continuous quality improvement, professional socialization, level of nursing, preparation for professional nursing roles, professional organizations.

2. Nursing education issues: Basic nursing education and advanced education for nurses; competency-based education; preparation for specialty and advanced nursing practice, continuing nursing education.

3. Health care policy: Changing health care context and trends, health care reform, health care economics, health-care delivery models, the organization and management of health care, political participation, advocacy in nursing, participation in health care policy, nursing workforce issues.

4. Health and nursing informatics: Computer-aided learning, telehealth, systems available for hospital information, quality assurance, client assessment and monitoring, safety and security of information systems, protection of personal data, ethical and legal considerations in the use of information technology.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Nursing Research
courses.description: This course helps students to understand the principles of nursing research and equip their knowledge of evidence based nursing. Emphasis will be given to introducing students to the use of qualitative and quantitative research methods, research design and research questions in practice to enable students to carry out a critical review of the literature in a particular area of clinical practice.
courses.learning_outcomes:
At the end of this course, students will be able to:
1. Discuss the contribution of nursing research to the development of evidence-based practice.
2. Differentiate the major assumptions, characteristics and approaches between the positivist and naturalist paradigms in developing nursing science.
3. Develop a workable research problem in a selected area of clinical practice.
4. Discuss the purposes and methods of conducting literature review for quantitative and qualitative researches.
5. Discuss the characteristics, applications, strengths and weakness of various study design for quantitative and qualitative researches.
6. Discuss the characteristics, applications, strengths and weakness of various sampling methods and approaches for data collection in quantitative and qualitative researches.
7. Understand the principles and applications of quantitative and qualitative data analyses.
8. Discuss the ethical implications of conducting research in clinical practice settings.
9. Critically appraise a research article in a selected area of clinical practice.
10. Evaluate the significance of a research study in advancing nursing practice.
11. Describe the concepts and principles of integrating participatory approach in nursing research and discuss its value-adding potential in promoting evidence-based nursing practice.
courses.course_syllabus:
Evidence-based practice in nursing profession & overview of the research process:
Overview of the role of evidence-based practice in nursing; Two key methodological perspectives in nursing science; The major steps in a quantitative and qualitative studies; The similarities & differences on the research process of quantitative and qualitative study.

Research problems, research questions & hypothesis:
The process of developing a research problem; The different purposes and characteristics of problem statements, statement of purpose, research questions and research hypotheses; Formulation of research questions and research hypothesis from problem statement and statement of purpose.

Searching and reviewing the literature:
The purpose of a literature review; The appropriate information to include in a review of the literature; The methods of writing a literature review and of searching for existing literature for a research problem; The process of writing a literature review; Evaluation for the adequacy and quality of a literature review.

Quantitative research: study designs and issues of validity:
The different types of experimental, quasi-experimental and non-experimental study designs, and their key characteristics; The purposes, strengths and weakness of each type study designs; The application of each type study designs in nursing research; Internal and external validity related to study design;

Quantitative research: sampling designs:
Definitions of a population and a sample; The considerations in setting eligibility criteria; The rationales for sampling in research studies; The advantages and disadvantages of various sampling methods; The general considerations in estimating sample size in quantitative study; Evaluation for the sample method in a research study.

Quantitative research: data collection method:
Overview of different formats of question and scaling techniques; The use of observational methods and biophysiological measures in collecting data for quantitative research; Levels of measurement among quantitative data.

Quantitative research: issues of reliability & validity on data collection:
Overview of the major sources of measurement error; Concepts and dimensions of validity and reliability; Methods for evaluating reliability and validity.

Quantitative research: data analysis:
Overview of using descriptive statistics in nursing research; The basic idea of hypothesis testing and the logic of tests of statistical significance; The general principles in identifying a statistical test for research hypothesis; Type I and Type II errors and their roles in hypothesis testing; Methods of data coding and data entry in quantitative studies.

Ethical issues in research:
Overview of ethical issues in conducting a research; Main requirements for ethical approval.

Qualitative research: design & approach - Introduction to participatory action research in health care:
The purposes and methods of different types of qualitative research design; Application of qualitative research in nursing research. Introduction to participatory action research in health care.

Qualitative research: sampling designs & data collection method:
The importance of sampling; The criteria for judging the appropriateness and adequacy of sampling; Different methods of sampling; Issues in the sampling process.

Qualitative research: data analysis and issues of validity and reliability:
Common data analysis strategies used in qualitative research; Specific data analysis procedures used in qualitative approaches; Issues of validity and reliability in qualitative research.

courses.description: This course aims to draw upon the foundations of psychological and sociological concepts from a health and illness perspective, which is applicable to nursing practice in Hong Kong. With these concepts, students will examine the psychological and social determinants, which can be used to promote health, prevent illness and facilitate nursing interventions for individuals and families.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Discuss the influence of health beliefs on people’s practice of health-related behaviour.
2. Analyze the impact of illness and hospitalization on individuals, their family and society.
3. Analyze the application of health psychology to health promotion and disease prevention.
4. Critically debate the main issues in the social organizations of health care.
5. Examine the social and cultural explanations of health and illness in relation to dimensions of social inequality including gender, ethnicity and class.
6. Compare and contrast professional and lay response to the understanding of health and illness.

- Social diversities in health
- Gendered perspectives in nursing care

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 406
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 406

courses.description: This course aims to provide a theoretical foundation to community-based nursing and gerontology nursing. Emphasis will be placed on home-based assessment and management of common health problems in various community settings. This course enables students to formulate appropriate nursing care plan and apply evidence-based nursing interventions to community nursing and to the care of older people. Basic concepts of public health, epidemiology and fundamental pharmacology will be included.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Apply basic principles of community nursing and home-based nursing in the discussion of the specific conditions in the community.
2. Demonstrate the ability to systematically identify and analyse the scope for gerontological care for the promotion of the health and wellness of older people in various settings.
3. Understand the basic concepts of public health and epidemiology.
4. Describe principles of basic pharmacological nursing.
courses.course_syllabus:
Major concepts and topics to be covered:

Community nursing:
Principles of community nursing
Development and trends of community nursing in Hong Kong
Roles and responsibilities of the community nurse
Common problems and management skill of community nursing
Planning and preparing for home visits
Basic concepts of public health and epidemiology
Family-centered care and community care
Family/home-based assessment and care planning
Home care nursing and managing specific conditions at home
Client and family education in home settings
Emotional problems and stress in the home
Prevention and management of violence and aggression in the community
Community and social support for older adults
Coordinating medical, health and social services to meet the clients & families needs
Community support and network for client in the community

Gerontological nursing:
Epidemiology of aging
Pathophysiology ,theories of aging and the normal aging process,
Philosophy of gerontological nursing and scope of care
Comprehensive health assessment in older people\_Functional assessment
Cognitive & neurosensory assessment in older adult
Cognitive function disorder: confusion, dementia
Common physical, and mental health problems and their management of older adults
Meeting physical needs and psychosocial needs of older people
Addressing psychological problem & stress for older adults
Homecare nursing & managing specific condition for older people (under nutrition, confusion, AOM consideration)
Mobility problem in older adult & fall prevention
Prevention of incontinence.
Geriatric rehabilitation
Inter-disciplinary approach in the care of older people
Health, social and residential care services for older people
Legal and ethical implications in gerontological care
End of life issues

Care plan in Community & Gerontological nursing:
Reinforce nursing care plan & nursing diagnosis in community nursing & gerontological nursing

Fundamental pharmacology:
basic concepts of pharmacokinetics.
major drug classifications and drug effects.
forms of medication and client care applications.
safety in drug therapy and clients care.
the related laws and ordinances.

Skills:
Cognitive & neurosensory assessment
Bags technique in community nursing

courses.title: Nursing in the Hospital I

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 402
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 402

courses.description: This course provides the theoretical basis for comprehensive nursing care to clients in the hospital with mild to moderate health problems. The pathophysiological basis and pharmacological treatment of medical-surgical nursing conditions will be examined together with the principles of therapeutic management and nursing therapeutics skills. Students will learn to assess, plan and implement appropriate evidence-based nursing interventions to reduce or overcome the effects of these conditions.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Formulate a nursing care plan appropriate to the health condition identified in the nursing assessment process.
2. Demonstrate ability to perform nursing skills safely and efficiently in the laboratory and clinical settings.
3. Develop an awareness of using established outcome measures to assess and document the effects of nursing interventions.
4. Explain the biopsychosocial dimensions of nursing practice, including the pathophysiological basis and body adaptation to the disease process, client education and psychosocial support to clients and family.
5. Apply the basic principles of pharmacological interventions in clinical nursing practice.
6. Apply principles and research findings from the biological, behavioural and nursing sciences in the care of clients with:
- Respiratory disorders
- Oesophageal disorders
- Gastrointestinal disorders
- Gall bladder, pancreatic and hepatic disorders
- Pre- and post-operative care of common surgeries
courses.course_syllabus:
Application of nursing assessment tools in clinical practice:
Identification of nursing problems through the use of nursing assessment tools
Formulation of appropriate NANDA approved nursing diagnoses
Formulation of realistic client-centred goals and measurable outcomes
Planning and implementation of appropriate nursing interventions in accordance with established rationale

Nursing care of clients with common health problems in hospitals (to include pathophysiological changes and body adaptation and pharmacological
interventions):
Respiratory disorders
Gastrointestinal disorders
Gall bladder disorders
Pancreas disorders
Hepatic disorders
Enteric infections
Surgical procedures

Nursing skills:
Oxygen therapy, oropharyngeal and nasopharyngeal suctioning, steam inhalation and metered dose inhalers administration, airway insertion, breathing exercise and coughing exercises, tracheostomy care and suctioning, chest drain, pulmonary assessment, ambu-bag manual ventilator, artificial airway, non-invasive ventilation (BiPAP), mechanical ventilation, abdominal assessment, initiating and monitoring total parenteral nutrition (TPN) through central venous catheter, colostomy and ileostomy care.

courses.description: This course further provides the theoretical basis for comprehensive nursing care to clients in the hospital with mild to moderate health problems. The pathophysiological basis and pharmacological treatment of medical-surgical nursing conditions will be examined together with the principles of therapeutic management and nursing therapeutics skills. Students will learn to assess, plan and implement appropriate evidence-based nursing interventions to reduce or overcome the effects of these conditions.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Formulate a nursing care plan appropriate to the health condition identified in the nursing assessment process.
2. Demonstrate ability to perform nursing skills safely and efficiently in the laboratory and clinical settings.
3. Develop an awareness of using established outcome measures to assess and document the effects of nursing interventions.
4. Explain the biopsychosocial dimensions of nursing practice, including the pathophysiological basis and body adaptation to the disease process, client education and psychosocial support to clients and family.
5. Apply the basic principles of pharmacological interventions in clinical nursing practice.
6. Apply principles and research findings from the biological, behavioural and nursing sciences in the care of clients with:

- alterations in reproductive functions
- alterations in genito-urinary functions
- alterations in haematological functions
courses.course_syllabus:
Application of nursing assessment tools in clinical practice:
• Identification of nursing problems through the use of nursing assessment tools
• Formulation of appropriate NANDA approved nursing diagnoses
• Formulation of realistic client-centred goals and measurable outcomes
• Planning and implementation of appropriate nursing interventions in accordance with established rationale

Principles of peri-operative nursing:
• General pre-operative care
• General post-operative care and prevention of post-operative complications
• Psychosocial and physiologic impact of surgery

Operating theatre nursing:
• Preparation and use of the operating theatre and annexes
• Preparation, sterilization, utilization and care of surgical instruments/equipments
• Types of anesthesia, principles of application and possible complications
• Commonly used anesthetic agents and related nursing implications
• Roles and responsibilities of the nurse in the operating theatre
• Skill of scrubbing up, gowning and donning of sterile gloves
• Care of the client prior to, during and after anesthesia
• Care of the client in the recovery room
• Intraoperative nursing care

Nursing skills: Surgical skin preparation, skills for operating theatre: surgical handwashing, scrubbing, donning a
theatre mask, gowning, and donning sterile gloves (open & closed methods), trolley setting in OT, care of
instruments/equipments in OT, post-operative exercises and ambulation, applying anti-embolic stockings,
nonpharmacological aids to promote comfort.

Nursing care of clients with common health problems in hospitals (including pathophysiological changes and body
adaptation and pharmacological interventions):

Alterations in musculo-skeletal functions:
• Infection: osteomyelitis, pyogenic arthritis, tuberculosis spondylitis, gangrene
• Chronic non-infective & degenerative conditions: osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, gouty arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis
• Trauma: fractures, dislocations, strains & sprains
• Metabolic diseases: osteoporosis, osteomalacia
• Neoplasm: bone tumor
• Congenital & developmental disorders
• Nursing skills: neurovascular assessment, applying a stump bandage, care of client immediately after cast application, continuous care after cast application, applying non-adhesive skin traction, assisting a client in traction, pin-tract dressing, turning clients with orthopaedic conditions

Alterations in genito-urinary functions:
• Kidney and bladder conditions: acute glomerulonephritis, nephrotic syndrome, renal failure, pyleonephritis, renal calculi, TB kidney, traumatic conditions, polycystic kidney, urinary tract infection, calculi, neoplasm
• Prostate conditions: benign prostatic hypertrophy, prostate cancer
• Nursing skills: continuous bladder irrigation (closed system), intermittent peritoneal dialysis, continuous ambulatory peritoneal dialysis

Alterations in haematological functions:
• Anaemias
• Haemorrhagic conditions: thrombocytopenia and hemophilia
• Leukaemias
• Autoimmune diseases: SLE
• Human immunodeficiency infection (HIV)
• Malaria
• Nursing skills: administering a blood transfusion

Alterations in reproductive functions:
• Physiological disorders: menstrual disorders, displacement of uterus and vagina, infertility and subfertility, endometriosis, menopause
• Inflammatory and infective conditions: vulvitis, vaginitis, pelvic infection, mastitis
• Neoplasm: vulva, cervix, uterus, ovary and breasts
• Abnormal pregnancy: abortion, ectopic pregnancy, molar pregnancy
• Nursing skills: assisting vaginal examination, taking vaginal swab, performing vaginal irrigation, insertion and removal of vaginal packs, insertion and removal of vaginal pessaries, application of vaginal medications, contraceptive methods

courses.description: This course aims to develop students’ understanding of legal and ethical issues in relation to nursing. It focuses on the legal principles governing professional practice in Hong Kong including torts, contracts, confidentiality, informed consent and related ordinances; basic ethical principles and theories in relation to nursing practice; and ethical issues and dilemmas encountered in practice.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Recognise contemporary ethical issues in nursing practice.
2. Examine common ethical dilemmas within the context of nursing practice.
3. Identify different approaches to ethical decision-making.
4. Apply ethical principles and theories in analyzing the dilemmas.
5. Explain basic legal concepts and their relevance to nursing and health care.
6. Examine the principles of tort law and the Hong Kong Ordinances as related to nursing.
courses.course_syllabus:
Ethical aspects:
- Bioethical principles and approaches and ethical decision-making models
- Code of conduct, professional ethics and organizational constraints in nursing practice

Legal aspects:
- The nature of law
- The legal system in Hong Kong
- Ordinances and principles of tort laws relevant to nursing; Negligence; Malpractice; Assault and battery; Informed consent; Defamation; Confidentiality; Documentation; Nurses Registration Ordinance; Pharmacy and Poison's Act; Dangerous Drug Act and Mental Health Ordinance.
- Integrity in practice

Case studies:
- Ethical and legal issues involved in truth telling, withholding and withdrawing treatment, euthanasia, abortion, organ transplant and research ethics

courses.course_syllabus:
Mental Health Nursing
• Principles of mental health nursing; roles and responsibilities of a mental health nurse
• Common mental health disorders: classification, etiology, and signs and symptoms
• Structure of mental health services in Hong Kong
• Nursing care and management of clients with common kinds of mental health problems
• Psychological and mental status investigations and assessments
• Psychological treatment and use of psychotropic drugs
• Rehabilitation of clients with mental health problems and community care
• Legal and ethical issues in mental health nursing care; and Mental Health Ordinance (Chapter 136, the Laws of Hong Kong)

Medical & Surgical Nursing
Apply research evidence and principles of nursing management in planning care of clients with:
• Cardiac disorders: coronary heart disease, valvular heart disease, carditis, rheumatic fever, rheumatic heart disease, and heart failure
• Vascular conditions: hypertension, varicose veins, peripheral vascular disease, aneurysm, arteriosclerosis, atherosclerosis, and phlebotomy
• Severe cardiac disorders: Asystole, pulseless ventricular tachycardia or fibrillation, bradycardia, tachycardia, complete heart block.
• Team approach in resuscitation in the hospital and airway management.

Skills:
• Cardiac and peripheral assessment; central venous pressure; and ECG care and interpretation
• ACLS team approach.

Alterations in lymphatic functions:
• Lymphoma, lymphadenitis and Hodgkin’s disease

courses.description: This course aims to continuously further equip students with the knowledge and skills to care for clients with moderate to severe health problems. It focuses on the physiological and/or pathophysiological changes, the application of nursing interventions, critical thinking, and research evidence to caring for clients in different clinical specialties.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Apply the framework of functional health patterns in studying the physical and biopsychosocial needs of clients with medical-surgical conditions.
2. Perform comprehensive health assessment and focus assessment of clients with altered functional health patterns.
3. Critically analyze assessment data to diagnose actual and potential alterations in functional health patterns.
4. Apply the knowledge of pharmacology in care of clients with medical-surgical conditions.
5. Formulate nursing care plans for clients with endocrine, neurological and dermatological disorders.
6. Apply interventions in caring for clients with travel-related communicable diseases.
7. Integrate research evidence in the care of clients and families experiencing moderate to severe health problems.
courses.course_syllabus:
Alterations in endocrine functions:
• Pituitary gland disorders: gigantism/acromegaly, Simmond’s disease, diabetic insipidus, dwarfism, neoplasms
• Thyroid gland disorders: congenital abnormalities, hyperthyroidism,
goiters, hypothyroidism, neoplasms
• Parathyroid gland disorders:
hyperparathyroidism, hypoparathyroidism
• Pancreas disorders: diabetes mellitus, pancreatic cancers
• Adrenal gland disorders: Cushing’s syndrome, Addison’s disease,
Pheochromocytoma
• Nursing skills: Education of home management for patients with
diabetes mellitus

Alterations in neurological functions:
• Traumatic neurological disorders: head injuries and spinal cord injury
• Cerebral vascular conditions: cerebral vascular accidents, cerebral aneurysm and arterio-venous malformations
• Degenerative neurological disorder: Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis, myasthenia gravis
• Seizure disorders
• Infectious neurological disorders: meningitis, encephalitis, brain abscess, rabies, tetanus
• Migraine and neurological-related sleep disorder
• Neurological neoplasms
• Cranial nerves disorder
• Nursing skills: neurological physical assessments and diagnostic test

Alterations in dermatological functions:
• Eczema: contact dermatitis, atopic eczema
• Bacterial infection: folliculitis, furuncles, carbuncles
• Fungal infections: tinea infections, candidiasis
• Viral infections: herpes, warts
• Parasitic infestations: scabies, pediculosis (covered in year 1 NRS1053 Fundamentals of Nursing, in 2009-2010)
• Acne
• Psoriasis
• Skin cancers and neoplasms
• Burns and scalds

Laboratory
Lecture

9. Further develop critical thinking and clinical judgment from theoretical knowledge and practical experience.

courses.description: This course will enable students to extend their theoretical and skill-based knowledge in co-coordinating individual client care and organising the care groups of clients in both hospital and community settings. 5. Discuss strategies to manage crisis successfully.
6. Analyze theories of change and their implications for nursing practice.
7. Discuss the roles of client care manager and unit manager.
8. Develop the ability with professional development to contribute as citizens and leaders.

courses.description: This course aims to integrate practice with theory to understand the nature of caring for individuals across developmental stages that are at risk or who have complex health problems. The course helps students to explore factors contributing to the complexity of changes in health. The foci will be on the physiological and/or pathophysiological changes, the application of nursing interventions, critical thinking, and research evidence to caring for clients with different complex health needs.
courses.learning_outcomes:
In regard to the care of child, adult and elderly clients with complex health problems, students will be able to:
1. Apply a selected framework in studying the bio-psychosocial care of clients who have developed complex health problems across the life span.
2. Contrast between the health assessment and nursing care for patients across the life-span.
3. Conduct a critical analysis on assessment data to diagnose actual and potential alterations in functional health problems.
4. Design and implement a holistic, accurate and evidence-based nursing care plan at an individual and family level for patients with complex health needs.
5. Prioritize client care considering life threatening situations as well as client preference.
courses.course_syllabus:
Oncology and palliative nursing
• Principles of oncology nursing.
• Principles of radiation therapy and chemotherapy.
• Care of clients undergoing radiation therapy and chemotherapy.
• Occupational safety in radiation therapy and chemotherapy.
• Pain and symptom management.
• Principles of palliative care.
• Legal and ethical issues.

Rehabilitative nursing
• An overview of rehabilitative care: principles of rehabilitation, comprehensive assessment, family-centered care, roles and responsibilities of rehabilitative nurses.
• Principles and programs in cardiac rehabilitation, neurological rehabilitation, pulmonary rehabilitation, and geriatric rehabilitation.
• Framework of care in cardiac rehabilitation, neurological rehabilitation, pulmonary rehabilitation and geriatric rehabilitation.
• Principles of planning, implementing and evaluating nurse-led initiatives for patients with chronic illness.

Obstetric Nursing
• Principles of care in normal pregnancy, labour and puerperium.
• Physiological and psychological changes in pregnancy.
• Common complications of pregnancy, labour and puerperium.
• Newborn care (including neonates, preterm and small for dated babies), breastfeeding versus bottle-feeding.
• Postpartum adaptation & family visit.
• Introduction to obstetric emergencies.
• Roles and responsibilities of midwife.

Skills:
• Antenatal assessment
• Nursing interventions to promote comfort during child birth
• Newborn care

courses.description: This clinical practice course aims to provide opportunities for students to identify and analyse the effect of complex changes in health and the impact of multiple health agencies on clients. Students are expected to demonstrate competence in the delivery of holistic care for clients with complex health problems and to promote health across different age groups. Additionally, under the supervision of experienced nurses, students are required to demonstrate responsibility for their own decision making about nursing interventions, evaluating practice and managing care for a group of clients. Students are expected to work collaboratively with the health-care team in the delivery of care to clients.

3. Demonstrate proficiency in using the nursing process as a problem-solving approach in the provision of client-centred care.
4. Demonstrate competence in family nursing for geriatric clients.

7. Demonstrate effective communication with clients, their families, and health care team members.

10. Demonstrate competence in the safe and efficient performance nursing skills throughout clinical practice.
11. Demonstrate the proficiency in using reflective skills for self and peer appraisal of nursing care.
12. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of lifelong and ongoing professional development.

10. Demonstrate competence in the safe and efficient performance nursing skills throughout clinical practice.
11. Demonstrate the proficiency in using reflective skills for self and peer appraisal of nursing care.
12. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of lifelong and ongoing professional development.

courses.feedback_evaluation:
The course adopts a number of formal and informal measures to generate feedback for evaluation about the course. Students are encouraged to use the CU eLearning System and email to post your views, and class representatives are invited to collate the views of students during and at the end of the course. Another means of evaluation is using Course and Teaching Evaluation to evaluate the course. There are also opportunities for students to feedback about the course, lectures, tutorials and lab days during the staff-student consultative committee. During the lectures, tutorials and lab days, students are welcomed to approach the teaching staff to give their feedback as well.

Students can review their assessment materials. The review period will be held within two weeks after grades are released to students. The date will be determined by the Course Coordinator. Student who wishes to review the assessment materials must make a formal request to the Year Administrator within four calendar days after grades are released.

courses.title: Scholarly Inquiry
courses.description: This course aims to equip students with knowledge and skills to critically appraise literature and summarise evidence to improve nursing practice and health outcomes of individuals and populations. This will involve analysing the contribution of research and the appropriateness of the research approaches to knowledge development.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. analyse the processes for establishing current evidence for specific areas of nursing practice.
2. identify the benefits and limitations of evidence-based practice.
3. critically review the literature that underlies a selected healthcare topic.
4. evaluate the designs and methods commonly used in research and service innovation project.
5. discuss the quality of measurement in both quantitative and qualitative research.
6. develop a critical review of the literature in a selected area of a nursing topic.

courses.title: Contemporary Issues in Advanced Nursing Practice
courses.description: This course aims to enable students to expand knowledge and understanding of nursing practice in a diverse context. The course covers theoretical foundation of nursing practice, social determinants of health in relation to various populations including children, adolescents, older people and families, healthcare policy, healthcare financing, healthcare system organisation, human diversity and social issues that influence nursing practice. The course also enables students to discuss strategies, approaches and interventions to address the emerging health issues.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. examine the fundamental concepts, beliefs and values of nursing models in the context of contemporary healthcare environment.
2. describe health equity issues and specific health needs of different populations.
3. discuss the professional role of nurses and the scope of nursing practice in a diverse context.
4. critically appraise the recent healthcare policy initiatives, healthcare financing and healthcare system organisation, and their impacts on population health.
5. discuss strategies, approaches and interventions to health promotion, disease prevention and health restoration.

courses.description: The course aims to provide students with an advanced level of knowledge in statistics to enable them to appropriately apply such knowledge for evaluating nursing innovation in the complex healthcare context. It also enables the students to have an advanced level of statistical literacy to appraise the science in nursing and health care. The course covers regression modelling, psychometric evaluation methods, repeated measures, factors analysis, and structural equation modelling.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing and Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. develop the knowledge and skills to read, critically evaluate, and discuss the statistical methods in scientific nursing literature.
2. critically discuss the methods and considerations in identifying appropriate statistical methods for evaluating an evidence-based nursing practice.
3. develop the knowledge and skills in conducting a variety of statistical techniques for evaluating the effects of an evidence-based nursing practice.
4. develop the knowledge and skills in adopting a variety of statistical techniques to estimate sample size required for evaluating an evidence-based nursing practice.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Scholarly Inquiry Project I
courses.description: This course facilitates students to develop a scholarly inquiry project in promoting evidence-based practice through knowledge development and transfer. Students are expected to utilise the research findings to meet the changing healthcare needs and to enhance the quality of care. They are required to apply the knowledge they have learned in this master’s programme to complete the scholarly inquiry project. In addition, reflection on the process of developing research methods or project plans, and the strategies adopted to overcome difficulties during the process will be shared and discussed.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. discuss the existing nursing practice in a selected clinical setting.
2. demonstrate critical thinking skills in appraising the relevant evidence to address the service/ knowledge gap.
3. initiate innovations through a research/ service innovation project approach to enhance advanced practice in a selected clinical setting.
4. describe the research method/ project plan developed for their scholarly inquiry project, and present the innovative materials if applicable.
5. identify the strengths and weaknesses of the selected research method/ innovative service.
6. critically discuss the significant issues related to their own and their peers’ research methods/ project plans.

5. Describe nursing responsibilities in preparing and assisting the client to undertake diagnostic procedures.
6. Apply the basic principles of pharmacological interventions in clinical nursing practice.

courses.description: This course aims to provide students with basic knowledge of human topographical anatomy with emphasis on those aspects of special signifi cance in the nursing practice. The course will partially run concurrently with that of physiology. The syllabus will be integrated so that students will be able to correlate structure and function.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Understand the different cell and tissue types of the body.
2. Understand the importance of cell and tissue structures to their functions.
3. Be acquainted with the general organization of the organs in the body and how they organize to form a functional system.
4. Learn the importance of the structural relationship and arrangement in the clinical setting.
5. Describe both the macroscopic and microscopic structures of the organs, and relate them to the functions of the organs.
6. Learn the origin and development of the organs and the associated birth defects.

courses.description: The aim of the course is to enhance students' knowledge and understanding of how the body adapts physiologically to internal and external environmental changes. It focuses on the physiological principles of normal cells, tissues, and system functioning which will form the basis for studying health promotion, pathology, and nursing care. The course content will be partially integrated with that offered in Anatomy.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Describe the electrical properties of a neuron and understand the physiological significance of transmission of nerve impulse.
2. Explain how a skeletal muscle contracts and recognize the importance of neuromuscular transmission in a human body.
3. Discuss how the cardiovascular system helps regulate blood perfusion in body tissues.
4. Describe and explain how homeostasis helps maintain physiological functions such as thermoregulation.
5. Summarize how some of the altered functions in the cardiovascular and pulmonary systems affect the body.
6. Explain how the pulmonary system and blood transport oxygen to tissues and carbon dioxide from the tissues.
7. Outline the roles of blood in hemostasis and immune defense.

courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Describe the processes by how the kidneys regulate the body fluid volume and its composition.
2. Understand how the gastrointestinal system carries out the digestion and absorption of ingested food.
3. Summarize the roles of various hormones and understand the mechanisms of their secretions

6. Outline how some of the altered functions in the kidneys, gastrointestinal, endocrine, and central nervous system affect the body
7. Summarize the general principles on how the tissues and organs work altogether to maintain homeostasis

courses.description: This course comprises of several parts, including: The development of the philosophy and knowledge base in nursing which enables students develop their understanding of its evolution; The application of technology in information processing for nursing practice and research, including the systems available for hospital information and nursing management; An integrated study of professional issues related to nursing that will enhance students' educational and professional self-direction.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Examine the factors influencing the future directions of nursing practice.
2. Understand the health and illness concept and the effect of socio-cultural factors on individual health.
3. Recognize the nursing process as a problem solving approach in the care of clients.
4. Explain the methods for assuring quality in health care by nurses and the health care team.
5. Compare and contrast different approaches to career ladders in nursing.
6. Examine the challenges in professional development of nursing in Hong Kong.
7. Compare and contrast different health system models.
8. Analyze the challenges of reforming Hong Kong’s health system.
9. Analyze the advantages and limitations of using computer applications in the management of information.
10. Examine the implications of information technology for practice, research and education.
11. Determine and apply the ethical and legal principles in the use of information technology.

Students are equipped with the principles of therapeutic management and nursing skills to assess, diagnose, and implement evidence-based nursing interventions. Overall, it aims to promote health outcomes of these clients.

courses.learning_outcomes:

1. Perform a comprehensive health assessment and focus assessment of clients with altered functional health patterns.

4. Apply nursing research findings in planning the care of clients with: first aid need, perioperative, OT care and patients with alimentary and musculoskeletal disorders.
5. Integrate psychosocial support and intervention in nursing care to clients and their families with acute and chronic illnesses.

courses.description: This course aims to develop students' critical thinking in the identifi cation of legal and ethical issues relevant to nursing. It will focus on: legal principles governing professional practice in Hong Kong, including torts, contract, confidentiality, informed consent, and related ordinances; basic ethical principles and theories related to the nursing practice; and ethical issues and dilemmas encountered in the practice.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Recognise contemporary ethical issues in nursing practice.
2. Examine common ethical dilemmas within the context of nursing practice.
3. Identify different approaches to ethical decision-making.
4. Apply ethical principles and theories in analyzing the dilemmas.
5. Explain basic legal concepts and their relevance to nursing and health care.
6. Examine the principles of tort law and the Hong Kong Ordinances as related to nursing.

courses.description: This course aims to provide a theoretical foundation to public health concepts and community nursing. Its emphasis is placed on understanding the public health principles as well as the community nursing assessment and management of common health issues. Furthermore, it aims to encourage critical thinking in assessments, formulation of appropriate nursing diagnoses, and application of evidence-based interventions during the planning and delivery of care. Overall, it is intended to promote individual and family health.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
After completion of the course, students will be able to:
1. Establish understanding of the public health and epidemiology concepts.
2. Promote people’s health in the community.
3. Identify the present community nursing resources and services in Hong Kong.
4. Understand the roles of community nurses in promoting the health of clients in the community.
5. Apply relevant nursing interventions and research evidence in caring for clients based in communities.
6. Justify home-based assessment data to diagnose actual and potential alterations in functional health problems of clients.
7. Integrate psychosocial nursing interventions for the care of clients and their caregivers in their respective communities.

courses.description: This course is designed to provide students with a sociological perspective on health and illness which is applicable to the nursing practice in Hong Kong. As a result, students will be able to understand different social sciences theories and concepts relevant to nursing and/or health care in general. Hence, the social forces leading to health promotion and illness prevention for individuals and families in society are examined.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Analyze concepts and ideas of health and illness in context.
2. Suggest effects of social groups and social institutions in shaping individuals’ health and illness behaviour.
3. Justify the effects of globalisation and political economy on health issues in society.
4. Synthesize sociological perspectives in understanding nursing and patient care.

courses.description: This course aims to introduce developmental physiological adaptations in women reproductive processes. This includes the knowledge of relevant anatomy and physiology, the process of pregnancy/postpartum, nursing care of fetus, neonate, and infancy with an emphasis on their implications in an evidence-based nursing practice.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Examine the basic concepts of obstetric nursing in the promotion of the health and wellness of the expectant family.
2. Apply family-centered care to the clients in normal pregnancy, labour and puerperium.
3. Discuss midwifery responsibilities in preparing and assisting the client and family to provide newborn care.
4. Describe principles and knowledge underlying the performance of essential skills.

courses.description: This course aims to develop students' understanding of a nurse's role in health promotion when working with individuals, groups, families, and communities. Its emphasis lies on the application of health education and promotion principles, as well as critiquing programmes relevant to local, current, and predicted health needs.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Examine the role of the nurse in undertaking health promotion with individuals, groups, families and communities.
2. Analyse current theories and models of health education and health promotion and their values in the delivery of such programmes.
3. Examine the contribution of health education to health promotion.
4. Analyse the changing pattern of community health and identify priorities and opportunities for health promotion.
5. Assess the learning needs of individuals, families, groups and communities.
6. Apply principles of health promotion to the implementation of programmes.
7. Evaluate the process and outcome of health promotion programmes for both the nurses and clients.

courses.description: This course will enable students to expand their theoretical and skills-based knowledge in coordinating individual client care and organizing care for groups of clients in both hospital and community settings. Emphasis will be on leadership and management skills to maximize clients' outcomes, and the importance of multidisciplinary teamwork. An introduction of theories and processes of health care policy-making will also be included.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Explain how leadership skills of motivating, influencing, negotiating, delegating, and supervising can improve the quality of patient care.
2. Apply effective communication and team building skills in the coordination of nursing practice.
3. Discuss strategies to manage conflict and/or complaints effectively.
4. Analyze the theories of change and their implications for nursing practice.
5. Evaluate different strategies in change management.
6. Describe health care systems and health economics.
7. Analyze health service innovations in response to changing social and health care needs in Hong Kong.
8. Explain politics and power in health care policy-making.

courses.description: The course is designed to promote the students' understanding of the development of advanced nursing practice and the roles of advanced practitioner. Also, the students will be expected to apply common advanced nursing knowledge, skills, and principles of scientific investigation to substantiate their clinical practice.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
courses.learning_outcomes:
After completion of the course, students will be able to:
1. Examine the importance of advanced nursing practice issues
2. Analyze the factors that influence future directions of nursing practice.
3. Explore methods in assuring the quality of health care provided by nurses and the health care team as a whole.
4. Apply commonly used advanced practice skills in caring for patients.
5. Demonstrate competence in management of patients with various conditions.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Clinical Practice Block II
courses.description: These clinical practice blocks aim to provide students with clinical practice opportunities in order to apply principles, concepts, and theories for the care of clients with surgical conditions. Students will be given the chance to practice in a hospital setting. Facilitators will assist them to develop clinical problem-solving skills and to apply a nursing care plan in clinical settings. This will assist students in their transition to become Registered Nurses. In other words, their integration into the health care team in clinical practice will be facilitated.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
Prerequisite: NURS6304
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Apply a problem-solving approach in the identification and management of biopsychosocial health-related problems.
2. Demonstrate the ability to perform comprehensive and focus assessment of clients with surgical and orthopaedic alterations.
3. Demonstrate the ability to plan, implement, and evaluate individualized nursing care.
4. Deliver nursing care in a professional manner that is sensitive to client diversity.
5. Demonstrate effective communication with clients, their families, and health care team members.
6. Demonstrate competency in safe and efficient performance of nursing skills in the clinical settings.
7. Integrate the knowledge of pathophysiology, clinical manifestations, treatment, and pharmacotherapy to care for clients with different health disorders.
8. Apply the principle of teaching and learning in planning and implementing health education and health promotion to clients.
9. Relate research findings to the provision of health promotion and nursing care in terms of process and outcomes.
10. Demonstrate accountability and apply ethical and legal considerations in nursing practice.
11. Begin to practice critical thinking and clinical judgment by applying theoretical knowledge into practical experience.
12. Develop skills in self and peer appraisal of nursing care through reflections.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Clinical Practice Block III
courses.description: This clinical practice block aims to provide students with clinical practice opportunities to apply principles, concepts, and theories for the care of clients with various specialty conditions. These conditions include: psychiatric, obstetric, those requiring surgical operation, and community nursing care. Students will be given the chance to practice in both community and hospital settings. They will be assisted in developing clinical problem-solving skills and the use of a nursing care plan in clinical settings. This will assist students during their transition to become Registered Nurses. In other words, their integration into the health care team in clinical practice will be facilitated.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For students in Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration)
Prerequisite: NURS6305
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will be able to:
1. Identify the roles of different discipline in the care of clients and their family members with complex health problem.
2. Apply problem-solving approach in the identification and management of biopsychosocial health-related problems of clients.
3. Demonstrate a comprehensive and focused health assessment of individuals. Critically analyze health assessment data to identify, prioritize problems, and develop plans of care supported by research evidences.
4. Demonstrate competence in the safe and efficient performance of nursing skills in various clinical specialties, and in preparation and aftercare for investigative procedures.
5. Demonstrate effective communication with clients, their families, and multidisciplinary health care team members.
6. In partnership with client, facilitate the client’s psychosocial adaptation to experiencing moderate to severe health problems.
7. Establish ability to identify research topics from theoretical knowledge and practical experience.
8. Develop critical thinking skills and clinical judgment from theoretical knowledge and practical experience.
9. Demonstrate skills in self and peer appraisals of nursing techniques and care through reflections.
10. Evaluate and document assessment, care planning and implementation of care.
11. Demonstrate skills of coordinating the care for a group of clients.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Applied Biological Science in Advanced Nursing Practice
courses.description: The course is deigned to integrate physiology, pathophysiology, genetics and clinical implications across the lifespan for advanced nursing practice. Recent scientific advances will be discussed with application to patient education and counselling, disease and symptom management, and to support clinical decision making for restoring and maintaining health.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing and Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. discuss the normal function of body systems and key structural and functional changes of major body systems across the lifespan.
2. identify and discuss pathophysiology of common disorders that affect main body systems and pathological process across the lifespan.
3. discuss genetic/genomic basis of health, illness, disease risk, and treatment response.
4. demonstrate the ability to apply knowledge in physiology, pathophysiology and genetics/genomics in health assessment, patient education and counselling, and therapeutic management of actual and potential health problems.
