courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Advanced Health Assessment and Diagnostic Investigation
courses.description: This course is designed to equip students with advanced clinical assessment skills for advanced practice. The course covers comprehensive assessment of individuals, interpretation of clinical data and diagnostic investigation, consideration of differential diagnosis and evidence-based diagnostic reasoning, clinical decision making process for identifying health problems, and clinical judgment through analysis of clinical findings.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing and Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. perform and document comprehensive health history and physical assessments in a selected area of health care.
2. compare and contrast the principles and techniques in advanced health assessment including physical, psychological, socio-cultural, family and community assessments.
3. critically analyse the measures of diagnostic accuracy in evidence-based diagnostic reasoning.
4. demonstrate the ability to interpret and explain the clinical significance of abnormal clinical data and the underlying mechanisms of disease.
5. demonstrate the ability to justify their clinical decisions by drawing upon theoretical principles, clinical findings and practical experience.

courses.subject: NURS
courses.title: Therapeutic Design and Management
courses.description: The course offers students with opportunities to apply their knowledge and skills related to clinical decision making in pharmacotherapeutic and clinical reasoning for developing a patient care plan to manage the identified health problems. The course covers advanced principles of clinical pharmacology and therapeutics of several common diseases or conditions, various disease management models, and how these guide students through the process of clinical reasoning and decision making, and the development of a client-centred care plan.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. advance knowledge of pharmaceutical, pharmacodynamic and pharmacokinetic properties of the medication.
2. critically analyse medication management in relation to individual’s characteristics and clinical conditions.
3. apply appropriate monitoring in assessing the impact and efficacy of the medication.
4. demonstrate ability to incorporate analysis of clinical and diagnostic data in the clinical decision making process.
5. critically appraise various disease management models in the care of clients with complex healthcare needs.
6. formulate a client-centred care plan to address complex healthcare needs according to various disease management models, taking into consideration the differential diagnosis and the interpretation of assessment and clinical data.
7. demonstrate the ability of providing education and counselling to the client/family regarding the medication regimens and care plan.

courses.description: This course aims to enable students to develop an advanced level of knowledge and skills in nursing leadership and healthcare management. The course covers examination of healthcare organisations, theories and methods for improving the quality, safety and outcomes of healthcare services, service planning, mentorship, transformational leadership and future nursing development in the local and global contexts.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing, Master of Nursing and MPhil-PhD in Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. develop personal vision, mission and values.
2. critically explore the concepts, theories, and innovative models in advanced clinical leadership and healthcare management.
3. critically discuss the core concepts, processes and strategies for developing an organisational culture.
4. demonstrate the ability to systematically and comprehensively analyse the complexities of service development, improvement and innovation.
5. demonstrate a detailed understanding of the approaches or techniques used to plan/ lead the development, implementation and evaluation of services.
6. demonstrate an appreciation of the dynamic changes and the need for transformational leadership and mentorship in nursing.
7. conceptualise the future of nursing from the local and global perspectives.

courses.description: The course aims to help students develop expertise needed for taking a leadership role in working with others within a team and collaborating with other teams. The course covers contemporary issues in healthcare professions, concepts and competencies for inter-professional collaboration, barriers and facilitators to achieving a model of collaborative practice. The course also enables students to discuss innovative opportunities to change current practice, and client-professional partnership models for transforming care.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing and Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. employ effective communication and collaborative skills in the development and implementation of practice models and guidelines, standard of care and health policy.
2. demonstrate the ability to lead interprofessional teams in the analysis of complex practice and organisational issues.
3. demonstrate the ability to collaborate with members of the healthcare team to provide and promote interprofessional client-centred care at individual, organisational and system levels.
4. demonstrate the ability to develop and maintain client’s control over decision making, assess the client’s commitment to the jointly determined plan of care, and foster personal responsibility for health.
5. critically appraise the implications of clinical partnership and collaboration in healthcare transformation.

courses.description: This course aims to enable students to utilise their acquired knowledge and skills to make accurate clinical judgments and decisions in the delivery of safe and effective care in a variety of healthcare settings. The course covers the impact of clinical reasoning and clinical decision making on patient outcomes and safety, decision making frameworks and tools, and steps in the process of clinical decision making. Students are provided with opportunities of using skills learned in this course to address the identified clinical situation to ensure safe and effective care implementation.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing, MPhil-PhD in Nursing and Master of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. critically discuss the concepts of clinical reasoning and clinical decision making.
2. critically discuss the impact of nurses’ clinical reasoning and decision making on patient outcomes and safety.
3. critically examine decision making frameworks and tools for providing effective and efficient care.
4. demonstrate the ability to go through the clinical decision making process and justify their clinical decisions based on theoretical principles, practical experiences and ethical stance.
5. evaluate decisions in terms of health outcomes of the patient and efficacy of healthcare services, with consideration from patient’s and family’s perspectives.

courses.description: The aim of this course is to prepare students for active roles and develop their competencies in nursing leadership, advanced clinical decision making and evidence-based nursing innovations for managing the complex healthcare environment in a global context. Students will be provided with an intensive overseas clinical shadowing experience in high-quality healthcare organisations. They will also be provided with opportunities and guidance in using reflective approach to optimise their learning outcomes through clinical exposure. By the end of this course, they will be able to conduct a critical discussion on how the advanced model in nursing service provision can be applied to the healthcare systems in their own countries.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. critically evaluate the integration of theory and practice in clinical setting.
2. analyse and evaluate different approaches and models of nursing leadership, clinical decision making and advanced nursing practice in clinical setting.
3. critically reflect, analyse, reconsider and examine their own clinical experiences relating to nursing leadership, clinical decision making and advance nursing practice.
4. critically evaluate the healthcare outcomes in relation to different approaches in nursing leadership, clinical decision making and advanced nursing practice.

courses.learning_outcomes:

courses.description:
This course covers the foundational principles of evidence-based practice and the critical scientific appraisal of evidence to guide advanced evidence-based reasoning and decision making for translation and application to practice. Students are expected to summarise the evidence to address the identified practice issue and to guide development of their project management plan.
In this course, each student is required to regularly meet with his/her supervisor who will provide necessary guidance and supervision in thesis writing and monitor the student’s academic progress.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing
courses.component:
Lecture
Seminar
Thesis Monitoring Course
Interactive Tutorial
Web-enhanced Teaching
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. critically discuss the role of nursing knowledge and science in clinical and translational research in nursing.
2. identify a clinical issue for the DNurs project and critically discuss its significance to inform advanced nursing practice and improve care delivery.
3. describe the purposes and methods of systematic review of a selected topic and state the significance of the review findings for quality improvement.
4. identify PICO, develop searching strategies and identify available evidence relevant to the topic.
5. critically appraise the literature relevant to the selected topic.
6. summarise the evidence to address the review objectives, and justify the need of developing a project management plan for quality improvement.

courses.description:
This course focuses on methodologies for planning, implementing and evaluating the DNurs project. Students are expected to develop a project management plan based on the results of nursing inquiry to address the identified clinical problem, community health concern or nursing educational issues.
In this course, each student is required to regularly meet with his/her supervisor who will provide necessary guidance and supervision in thesis writing and monitor the student’s academic progress.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing
courses.component:
Lecture
Seminar
Thesis Monitoring Course
Interactive Tutorial
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. identify the aims, objectives and expected outcomes of the project management plan.
2. critically analyse a range of theoretical/ methodological frameworks and apply to the chosen project.
3. develop a comprehensive project management plan including the details of the strategies, implementation process and evaluation methods, budget plan and resources.
4. demonstrate the ability to integrate the identified evidence into the project management plan to improve patient and system-level outcomes.
5. prepare their application under the direction of their supervisor using the guidelines of an appropriate clinical research and ethics committee.
6. identify funding sources to support health and health service projects and demonstrate the ability to prepare application for project grants.

courses.description:
This course is designed to assist students in the completion of their DNurs project. The course covers analysis of the results, determination of how the results can be used for quality improvement, and recommendations for practice and system change with consideration of factors promoting sustainability. The results of the project will be disseminated to the agency or community of interest. Students will be mentored in professional presentations and manuscript development for publication.
In this course, each student is required to regularly meet with his/her supervisor who will provide necessary guidance and supervision in thesis writing and monitor the student’s academic progress.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Doctor of Nursing
courses.component:
Seminar
Thesis Monitoring Course
Interactive Tutorial
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
1. analyse the collected data and evaluate the effectiveness of the project.
2. identify areas of success and areas for improvement in the project.
3. recommend strategies for practice or system change, and promote sustainability of the project.
4. disseminate the report to the agency or community of interest.
5. prepare a manuscript for publication.
